skill.name: impl
skill.description: Implements planned coding tasks from Plans.md, updates files, writes tests, and runs local validation commands for feature work.
skill.summary: Implements planned features and related tests from Plans.md tasks.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, weak-crypto, and obfuscation patterns. Review found the weak-crypto, obfuscation, path traversal, and most command matches are false positives from Markdown examples, front matter, Japanese prose, relative imports, and documentation links. The remaining real risk is legitimate but broad: the skill allows Bash plus file editing and recommends running project commands such as npm scripts.

medium_findings.title: Bash-Enabled Workflow Can Execute Project Scripts
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash and recommends commands such as npm test, npm run build, npm run dev, npx tsc, and shell pipelines. These are normal for implementation work, but project scripts can execute arbitrary local code and should require user awareness.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool list and documented commands directly show Bash usage. The risk depends on the target repository scripts, so this is elevated but not evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Broad File Modification Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to create and edit code, update Plans.md, and add test files. This is expected for a coding skill, but it can alter repository contents widely if the user request or task plan is unsafe.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The capability is explicit in tool permissions and output descriptions. It is a legitimate implementation feature, but it has meaningful filesystem impact.

medium_findings.title: Instruction Priority Assertion in Skill Content
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md states that one section has priority over other instructions during implementation. It appears focused on quality guardrails, not bypassing security review, but priority claims inside community skills should be treated cautiously.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text asserts priority, which is a prompt-injection-like pattern. Context indicates it is a quality rule, so confidence in malicious intent is low to medium.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Static Hits Are Mostly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Many external command findings point to fenced code blocks, templates, or example commands rather than hidden executable code. They document normal developer workflows such as reading Plans.md and checking tests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are visible Markdown examples and not embedded scripts. They remain relevant because the skill may ask an agent to run similar commands.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal and Device File Findings Are Benign Context
low_findings.description: The path traversal matches are a documentation link and relative TypeScript imports. The device-file match redirects errors from a listing command to /dev/null and does not access sensitive data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context shows links, imports, and shell redirection, not filesystem escape logic. No evidence found of reading or writing outside the workspace.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Obfuscation Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and entropy findings point to front matter, Japanese text, and Markdown content. No cryptographic implementation, encoded payload, or hidden binary content was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are natural language descriptions. Review did not find MD5, SHA1 usage, encryption routines, or obfuscated executable payloads.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution Through Bash
dangerous_patterns.description: Bash is an allowed tool and the documentation includes npm, npx, cat, grep, ls, and shell redirection examples. This is useful for coding work but can execute untrusted project scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in allowed tools and documented workflow. It is not hidden, but it requires caution in untrusted repositories.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository File Writes and Edits
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill explicitly writes implementation files, test files, and Plans.md updates. This is expected functionality, but marketplace users should understand the scope before enabling it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file modification behavior is explicitly documented. It is legitimate for this skill but materially affects user projects.

content.value_statement: Planned coding tasks often stall when implementation, tests, and validation are handled separately. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused feature work from Plans.md to completion.
actual_capabilities: Reads Plans.md task context and selects implementation or test-writing guidance.
Guides feature implementation with file creation, file editing, and task status updates.
Provides quality guardrails against hardcoded, stub, or test-only implementations.
Suggests validation commands for TypeScript builds, tests, and development servers.
Provides reusable patterns for React components, API routes, utilities, and tests.
Produces structured completion summaries with changed files and verification results.
limitations: Requires useful Plans.md tasks or clear user instructions to choose the right work.
Uses Bash and file-editing permissions, so untrusted repositories need extra caution.
Does not replace a dedicated security review or architectural review workflow.
Validation quality depends on the project test suite, scripts, and local tooling.

use_cases.title: Implement a Planned Feature
use_cases.description: Convert a selected Plans.md task into code changes, then update task status and report the result.

use_cases.title: Add Missing Tests After Coding
use_cases.description: Create focused tests for an implemented component, utility, or API route using the project test framework.

use_cases.title: Guide Junior Implementation Work
use_cases.description: Use the quality checklist to avoid stubs, hardcoded answers, and narrow fixes that only satisfy one case.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Planned Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: choose one task and implement it carefully.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the impl skill to inspect Plans.md, select the next TODO task, explain the implementation target, and make the required code changes.

prompt_templates.title: Implement With Tests
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: add code and tests in one focused pass.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the impl skill to implement the selected Plans.md feature, add appropriate tests, run the relevant validation command, and summarize the changed files.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: extend existing code while following local patterns.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the impl skill to find the existing implementation pattern, extend it for this feature request, avoid hardcoded behavior, and update Plans.md when complete.

prompt_templates.title: Deliver a Complex Feature Slice
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: coordinate implementation, testing, and risk checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the impl skill for this Plans.md feature. First identify affected files and security-sensitive areas, then implement, write regression tests, run validation, and report residual risks.

output_examples.input: Implement the next Plans.md task for the settings page.
output: Identifies the active settings task and marks it in progress.
Updates the relevant component and supporting utility files.
Runs the project validation command and reports pass or fail status.
Marks the task complete with a concise implementation summary.

output_examples.input: Add tests for the new utility function.
output: Detects the project test framework and existing test file layout.
Adds normal, boundary, and error-case coverage for the utility.
Runs the targeted test command and summarizes the result.

output_examples.input: Build the API route from the planned task and check security concerns.
output: Reviews the route requirements and related existing files.
Implements request handling with validation and error handling.
Calls out authentication or authorization checks that need human review.

best_practices: Review the selected Plans.md task before allowing file edits or command execution.
Run only the smallest validation command needed for the current change.
Ask for a summary of changed files, tests run, and remaining risks after each task.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to run broad project scripts in an untrusted repository without review.
Do not accept implementations that only return expected test values or stubs.
Do not combine unrelated Plans.md tasks into one implementation pass.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps implement planned coding tasks, add tests, run validation commands, and report completion details.

faq.question: Does it require Plans.md?
faq.answer: It is designed around Plans.md tasks, but a clear user request can provide similar context.

faq.question: Can it edit files?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill allows file creation and editing for implementation and test work.

faq.question: Can it run shell commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It allows Bash and includes examples for build, test, and inspection commands.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. Project scripts can execute arbitrary code, so review commands before running them.

faq.question: Is this a code review skill?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on implementation and test writing, not independent review or final approval.
